text.title: hybrid-search-implementation Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for production hybrid-search implementations across Weaviate, Qdrant, Pinecone, Elasticsearch.

rule.statement: For Weaviate, use the native hybrid() query method with alpha parameter; do not run dense + BM25 client-side when the server supports it.
rule.rationale: Weaviate handles BM25 preprocessing and fusion internally, reducing latency and code complexity vs client-side fusion.

rule.statement: For Qdrant, pre-compute sparse vectors (BM25 or SPLADE) in application code before insert; Qdrant stores (indices,values) but has no built-in tokeniser.
rule.rationale: Passing raw text to a sparse vector field produces a type error at the API level. Tokenisation must live in application code.

rule.statement: For Pinecone sparse-dense, train BM25Encoder on the full corpus before ingestion; significant corpus changes invalidate the encoder and require retraining.
rule.rationale: BM25Encoder learns corpus-wide IDF. Stale IDF weights degrade keyword recall as the corpus grows.

rule.statement: Default to RRF over linear combination when leg scores come on different scales; require an alpha sweep before switching to linear.
rule.rationale: RRF uses rank positions and is immune to score-scale mismatches. Linear without normalisation is fragile.

rule.statement: Measure alpha on a labeled eval set of ≥50 queries before deploying; never ship a guessed alpha to production.
rule.rationale: Wrong alpha can be worse than either method alone. Intuition about the right value is unreliable across corpora.

rule.statement: Pre-fetch 2-3x the desired top-K from each leg (e.g., k_leg=30 for final k=10) so fusion can promote middle-ranked documents that win across both methods.
rule.rationale: Documents that rank 8-15 in each leg but win in fused score are the entire reason hybrid beats single-leg. Fetching only top-K per leg loses them.

rule.statement: If the target store is not in {Weaviate, Qdrant, Pinecone, Elasticsearch} or latency budget blocks two-leg retrieval, skip hybrid-search-implementation.
rule.rationale: Covered backends define the implementation paths; outside them this methodology does not apply directly.
